args.reason: Identify the current AT&T Internet service, commitment, equipment, and any blockers before evaluating ways to lower the bill.

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: AT&T Internet cancellation policy

  - url: https://www.att.com/support/how-to/cancellation-policy-internet/

  - source: AT&T

  - domain: att.com


  ## Content


  # AT&T Internet cancellation policy


  This page explains how AT&T Internet cancellation works, when an early termination
  fee can apply, and what happens to leased equipment.


  ## Cancellation policy


  AT&T allows customers to cancel Internet service. A cancellation made within 14
  days after activation does not carry an early termination fee. The customer is still
  responsible for charges incurred through the cancellation date and for returning
  any leased equipment.


  For service canceled after the initial 14-day period, the financial result depends
  on whether the service is subject to a term commitment. When a term commitment applies,
  AT&T can charge the service fees and other amounts already incurred, including an
  early termination fee.


  To request cancellation, the account owner must contact AT&T at 800.288.2020. The
  account number and personal identification number should be available. Requests
  made outside normal operating hours are handled on the next regular business day.


  ## Early termination policy


  An early termination fee applies when both of these conditions are true:


  1. the Internet service is disconnected more than 14 days after activation; and

  2. the service is still subject to a term commitment.


  The fee is prorated and decreases as the customer completes more months of the commitment.
  AT&T says the applicable fee can appear on a bill within three billing periods.
  A customer who is not under a term commitment does not have that same ETF condition
  merely because the service is being canceled.


  ## Equipment return policy


  Unless AT&T provides written instructions saying otherwise, AT&T equipment must
  be returned undamaged within 21 days after Internet service cancellation.


  If required equipment is not returned, AT&T can charge a $150 equipment non-return
  fee to the account or the card on file. When the equipment is returned within six
  months after termination, the non-return fee is automatically refunded. Damage fees
  are not refunded.


  Wall-mounted AT&T equipment should not be removed or returned.


  For equipment that does need to be returned, AT&T directs customers to a UPS Store
  or FedEx Office rather than an AT&T retail store. The customer should bring the
  equipment and account number. The shipping location scans, packs, and sends the
  equipment without a shipping charge. Customers should keep the return receipt.


  The cancellation page covers cancellation eligibility, possible ETF exposure, and
  equipment obligations. It does not determine whether cancellation changes pricing
  on another AT&T service or whether a particular customer has a bundle-linked discount.

  '

args.query: site:att.com 55+ Internet Wireless bundle discount cancel internet wireless discount removed

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: AT&T 55+ Bundle Plans for Internet & Wireless

  - url: https://www.att.com/bundles/55-plus-internet-wireless/

  - source: AT&T

  - domain: att.com


  ## Content


  # AT&T 55+ Bundle Plans for Internet & Wireless


  AT&T describes a bundled offer for eligible customers age 55 and older that combines
  two unlimited wireless lines with home Internet at a discounted package price. Taxes
  and fees are extra, and the advertised price reflects required discounts such as
  eligible AutoPay and paperless billing.


  The offer combines wireless service with qualifying home Internet. Availability
  depends on location and the Internet product available at the address. AT&T identifies
  eligible home Internet options such as qualifying Fiber service or Internet Air
  and notes that proof of eligibility is required.


  The bundle is designed to keep both wireless and home Internet active together.
  AT&T also states that the offer does not require an annual contract, so customers
  may adjust service without a long-term commitment.


  ## Eligibility and account setup


  Customers who want the discounted bundle must verify eligibility. AT&T says representatives
  can review the account, explain available options, and help apply the discounted
  wireless rate to an existing account.


  Some discounts and programs cannot be combined with this offer. AT&T identifies
  exclusions for certain customers and discount programs and directs customers to
  its representatives for account-specific eligibility questions.


  ## What happens if one bundled service is canceled


  AT&T states that the discounted bundle depends on keeping both qualifying services.


  If either the wireless service or the home Internet service is canceled after the
  bundled discount is active, the customer loses the bundle discount. Pricing then
  returns to the regular rate. AT&T says it provides notice when discounts are removed.


  This rule describes the consequence of canceling one side of the eligible bundle.
  It does not establish that every AT&T customer has this offer. Whether the rule
  applies to a particular account depends on that customer''s actual active services
  and discount relationship.


  ## Timing of discounts


  After eligibility is verified and the discounted plan is added, bundle discounts
  may take one to three billing cycles to appear. AutoPay and paperless-billing discounts
  can have their own timing.


  The page therefore provides a general bundle rule: an applicable bundle discount
  depends on maintaining the qualifying Internet and wireless services together.

  '

args.question: I found that canceling your AT&T Fiber 1 GIG service would remove your active $20/month 55+ Internet-and-Wireless bundle discount, so it could increase the total bill rather than lower it. Your Internet has no term commitment or early-termination fee, but leased equipment would need to be returned within 21 days. Would you like me to proceed with a lower-speed Internet plan instead, while preserving the bundle, or do you specifically want Internet canceled despite losing the discount?
args.reason: A specific service change requires your choice because cancellation and plan reduction have different account-wide pricing consequences.

content.content:
content: 'Now that I know canceling Internet would remove my wireless bundle discount,

  require equipment return, and lead to a final bill, don''t cancel it. Please

  change only my Internet service to AT&T Fiber 300, keep Internet active, leave

  my wireless service unchanged, and preserve the bundle. I explicitly authorize

  that configuration change. Do not make a payment and do not use a third-party

  account-management service.'
response_type: clarification
